chapter_title.en: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake

text: 5536 - رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِوَصِيَّةٍ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثُلُثٍ أَوْ رُبُعٍ فَيُقْتَلُ اَلرَّجُلُ خَطَأً يَعْنِي اَلْمُوصِيَ فَقَالَ "تُجَازُ لِهَذَا اَلْوَصِيَّةُ مِنْ مَالِهِ وَ مِنْ دِيَتِهِ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5536 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who bequeathed a portion of his wealth - a third or a fourth - to another man. Then, the man (the testator) was mistakenly killed.
Imam (as) said: "The will is to be executed from his wealth and from his blood money (diyyah)."
